seo.title: Mino Translator | Bilingual YouTube Subtitles for Chrome
seo.description: Mino Translator is a Chrome extension for bilingual YouTube subtitles and in-page selection translation. Free Agnes AI recommended.
seo.ogDescription: Bilingual subtitles for YouTube videos and instant translation for selected text on any webpage.

hero.title: Watch YouTube in any language
hero.subtitle: Bilingual subtitles for videos, instant translation for any text you select, with a clear ZIP install guide.
hero.installTitle: Open the door to global content
hero.installText: Install Mino and any YouTube video can have subtitles in your language.

checks.title: Any language
checks.text: Japanese, Korean, Spanish, French — Mino translates into whatever language you speak.

checks.title: Syncs as you play
checks.text: Translations appear on screen, perfectly synced with the video.

checks.title: Background mode
checks.text: Start a translation, close the page, and get notified when it's done.

checks.title: Multiple at once
checks.text: Translate several videos simultaneously — each one runs independently.

features.title: There when needed, invisible when not
features.body: Three core features — subtitles, selection translation, and model setup — each doing its job without getting in your way.

items.title: Bilingual YouTube subtitles
items.body: Source and translated subtitles overlaid on the player, synced to playback position.

items.title: Selection translation
items.body: Select any text and a lightweight translation bubble appears. Read and move on.

items.title: Free model included
items.body: <agnesLink>Agnes AI</agnesLink> is free — sign up and grab your API key to get started. Also supports <models>_models_</models>, and any OpenAI-compatible model of your choice.

items.title: Quick install
items.body: Download ZIP, load into Chrome, and start using it immediately.

items.title: Customize the look
items.body: Font size, color, opacity, position — adjust the subtitles to your liking.

scenes.youtubeTitle: Watch the video, not the translation
scenes.youtubeBody: Bilingual subtitles overlay the video with adjustable opacity and font size. You focus on the content — Mino handles the translation.
scenes.youtubeAlt: Bilingual subtitles on a YouTube video player
scenes.popupBody: Open a captioned YouTube video and Mino detects available tracks automatically. Click the icon to open the panel, choose your target language, and start translating. A lightweight notification appears in the corner when done — no tab switching needed.
scenes.popupAlt: Extension popup showing an active translation task

download.title: Download and load into Chrome manually
download.body: Download the ZIP, unzip it, and load the unpacked extension in developer mode.

install.title: Install in four clear steps
install.body: Manual installation takes a few extra clicks — every step is laid out clearly.

steps.title: Download ZIP
steps.body: Get the latest package from the website.

steps.title: Unzip folder
steps.body: Extract it somewhere easy to find.

steps.title: Load unpacked
steps.body: Open chrome://extensions and enable developer mode.

steps.title: Add API key
steps.body: Enter API URL, API key, and model ID in settings.

api.title: Your key, your model
api.body: <agnesLink>Agnes AI</agnesLink> offers free credits — sign up, get your API key, and start using it. Also compatible with <models>_models_</models>, and any OpenAI-compatible API.
points: Free model — sign up and get your API key
Supports popular LLMs of your choice
API keys stay in local browser storage only

items.question: Where can I download Mino?
items.answer: The website provides a ZIP package with a detailed manual install guide.

items.question: Why do I need developer mode?
items.answer: ZIP installation requires enabling developer mode and loading the unpacked folder.

items.question: Do I need my own API key?
items.answer: Yes — you need an API key. Mino recommends Agnes AI which is free. Sign up, grab your key, and start translating. You can also bring your own OpenAI-compatible API provider.

items.question: Which languages are supported?
items.answer: Mino supports Japanese, Korean, English, French, Spanish, and more. The website UI supports Simplified Chinese and English.

items.question: Which websites are supported?
items.answer: YouTube is the core scenario for subtitles. Selection translation works on most common webpages.

items.question: Can it translate videos without captions?
items.answer: No. Subtitle translation depends on the video having a caption track.

items.question: Does the website receive my API key?
items.answer: No. All requests go directly to your configured API provider.

items.question: How do I update the extension?
items.answer: Download the new ZIP, unzip it, and reload the unpacked extension folder in Chrome.

items.title: No account needed
items.body: The website does not offer sign-up or login.

items.title: Text stays private
items.body: Translation text is never sent to the website.

items.title: Bring your own provider
items.body: Requests go directly to your configured API provider.

items.title: Local storage only
items.body: API keys are stored locally by the browser extension.
